pr.title: Prevent disposed dashboard menus from leaking
pr.author: JamesNK

failed_jobs.name: Tests / Hosting.PostgreSQL / Hosting.PostgreSQL (windows-latest)
failed_jobs.reason: Post-test steps (upload logs, copy CLI E2E recordings, generate summary) all failed with exit code -1073741502 (0xC0000142 STATUS_DLL_INIT_FAILED). Tests themselves passed (92/92). This is a known Windows runner transient infrastructure issue.
failed_steps: Copy CLI E2E recordings for upload

failed_jobs.name: Tests / Cli / Cli (macos-latest)
failed_jobs.reason: Test LaunchDetachedAsync_ReportsForkProcessExitCodeWhenChildExitsBeforeMonitorAndStartTimeIsUnavailable failed with a collection count mismatch. The PR only modifies dashboard files (AspireMenu.razor.cs and AspireMenuTests.cs), not CLI code. This test failure is unrelated to PR changes.

failed_tests.name: Aspire.Cli.Tests.Commands.AppHostLauncherTests.LaunchDetachedAsync_ReportsForkProcessExitCodeWhenChildExitsBeforeMonitorAndStartTimeIsUnavailable
failed_tests.error:
Assert.Collection() Failure: Mismatched item count
Collection:     ["Failed to start the AppHost."]
Expected count: 2
Actual count:   1
failed_tests.reason: The PR only modifies dashboard components (AspireMenu.razor.cs and AspireMenuTests.cs). This CLI test is in an unrelated area and fails with a timing-sensitive collection count mismatch, suggesting a race condition where the second expected message is not yet present.
